page: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-16 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the Tau T2D machine family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores, meaning there is one thread per core with no simultaneous multithreading. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance scoring in the top 25 percent of tested instances. Multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks, such as uncached read and write operations, fall within the average range. The instance also demonstrates strong text generation speeds for small language models, while medium model inference and general database operations perform at average levels.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation without hyperthreading provides predictable compute performance for general-purpose workloads. The lack of local NVMe storage and GPU hardware makes it less suitable for heavy storage-bound databases or deep learning training. However, the balanced resource profile makes it well-suited for workloads such as web serving, software compilation, text processing, and running small-scale language model inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-16 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped directly to 16 physical cores, providing a single thread per core, and is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory. The instance does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights strong single-core CPU performance and strong text generation speeds for small language models, while multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations remain in the average tier. This resource profile offers predictable compute performance without hyperthreading overhead. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, software compilation, text processing, and small-scale model inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-16 is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, delivering strong single-core performance for web serving and compilation.

tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC instance with sixteen physical cores and balanced memory, optimized for predictable single-threaded compute performance.

64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
Strong single-core CPU performance and small-model text generation speeds
No bundled local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and text processing workloads
